alt: A result passes through availability, entry, measurement, retention, reporting, and publication stages.
longDescription: Start with the target cases. An availability or survival filter determines which cases are reachable; recruitment and eligibility determine which enter; a measurement process creates recorded variables; loss and exclusion determine which records remain in the analysis; choices among outcomes and analyses determine what enters the report; and whole-study dissemination determines what reaches the visible evidence base. The corresponding threats are survivorship, selection, information or observer, attrition, selective outcome reporting, and publication bias.

edges.label: measurement and classification: information or observer
outcome and analysis selection: selective reporting
